title: [Feature]: Evaluate an OCI Cache Cluster sink
problem: OCI GoldenGate lists OCI Cache Cluster as a target-only Big Data connection. nats-sinks has a generic Redis sink backlog item, but it does not yet define whether Oracle-managed cache clusters need a dedicated OCI sink profile.
proposal: Research an OCI Cache Cluster sink profile that builds on Redis-compatible semantics where appropriate while documenting Oracle Cloud authentication, network placement, TLS, eviction risk, TTL behavior, and whether a cache can satisfy a durable sink contract. The design must fail closed if the target cannot provide durable custody comparable to a database, object store, or committed spool.
users: OCI operators and application teams that want low-latency event handoff into Oracle-managed cache infrastructure without mistaking cache acceptance for durable archival storage.
delivery_semantics: The design must explicitly state whether cache writes are durable enough for ACK gating. If not, the connector must be marked non-durable or must only be usable as a non-ACK-gated fan-out target once routing and fan-out semantics exist.
security: Connection secrets, TLS material, keys, labels, and payload data must not be logged. Network security lists, private endpoints, and least-privilege policies must be documented.
acceptance: Research determines whether OCI Cache Cluster can be a durable ACK-gated sink.
The profile documents TTL, eviction, persistence, clustering, and duplicate handling.
Configuration separates Oracle-managed cache from generic Redis endpoints.
Tests use fakes or local Redis-compatible services without network calls in unit tests.
Documentation warns operators when cache storage is not durable enough for critical custody.
tests: Add unit tests for configuration, key mapping, TTL validation, duplicate policy decisions, and temporary/permanent error classification. Live tests must be gated and isolated.
documentation: Document the OCI Cache Cluster evaluation under sink planning and update roadmap and security guidance.
closeout: Close after the research decision or implementation is released.
